memory.summary: With the project due Thursday, I can't just zone out for leisure now; I need to tackle that quick chore first to keep my momentum going.

desires: Maintain my reliability within cohort_3 so they don't have to pick up the slack for me later.
Clear this trivial chore immediately to stop the nagging feeling of unfinished business before I dive into the term project.
Protect my remaining energy reserves by resisting the urge to over-schedule my evening, given my existing sleep debt.

goals.description: Execute the quick_chore immediately to clear my mental clutter and stop the nagging feeling before I engage with the team.
goals.rationale: As someone who values relationships above all else, I cannot afford to be mentally scattered or resentful when interacting with cohort_3. This trivial task is a low-cost investment in my emotional availability for them later.

goals.description: Draft a rough outline for the term project to secure a baseline of progress, rather than waiting until Thursday.
goals.rationale: My planning horizon is long-term, so I need to start now, but my current energy reserves (6/10) and sleep debt mean I should not tackle this complex cognitive load right after the chore. I will address this once the immediate relational need (the chore) is met.
